vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on Backup gshadow File
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /etc/gshadow-, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0000 /etc/gshadow-

triage_decision.reason: Changing permissions on the backup gshadow file is a standard STIG hardening step. The remediation only modifies file mode bits and does not affect system boot, networking, or critical services, making it low risk.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; filesystems affected; Potential mis-typing of chmod command could leave file overly permissive
